text: If sunshine and low humidity are your love language, today's a keeper: mostly clear early, sunny highs right around 80 with a light southwest breeze, and the air's about as clean as it gets. High pressure is running the show through the end of the week, so there's no real hedge here, just enjoy it. Out on the lake, south winds 5 to 10 knots easing west with 1 to 2 foot waves make for an easy, comfortable day on the water, and at 71 the lake itself is finally warm enough to just jump in. Friday nudges up to the mid 80s with even lighter winds, another good beach or paddle day. The next real question mark is next week, when the forecasters expect showers and storms to return as a cooler pattern moves in, so enjoy this stretch while it lasts. For today, I'd plan any outdoor plans with total confidence, good on the water through the evening with no rush to come in before sunset near 8:30.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-23T11:05:00+00:00): Mostly Clear, 57F feels 57F, humidity 72%, wind S 3 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Sunny, with a high near 80. Southwest wind around 8 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 58. Southwest wind 3 to 8 mph.
  Friday: Sunny, with a high near 84. South wind around 8 mph.
  Friday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 60. South wind around 5 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-23T10:51:00+00:00):
  1. Dry and seasonably warm weather will continue through the end of the week as high pressure settles over the region.
  2. Showers and thunderstorms are likely to return to the region next week as a series of shortwaves cross the Northeast, ushering in seasonably cooler conditions by midweek.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 223 AM EDT Thursday... No significant changes were made with this forecast.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: South winds 5 to 10 knots, becoming west. Mostly sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Tonight: West winds 5 to 10 knots, becoming south. Clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Friday: South winds 5 to 10 knots. Sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Friday Night: South winds 10 knots or less. Clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 71F, level 95.75 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-23: highs [GFS 79, Euro 78, ICON 78] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 2%, Euro 10%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-24: highs [GFS 84, Euro 82, ICON 82] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 2%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-25: highs [GFS 84, Euro 86, ICON 85] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
AIR: AQI 27 Good (OZONE) — Expect generally good air quality across Vermont for Thursday, July 23rd.
SUN: rise 2026-07-23T05:30-04:00, set 2026-07-23T20:28-04:00, UV max 7.3
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 80, low 58: Plenty of sun. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 50s.
  high 84, low 61: Mostly sunny. Highs in the mid 80s and lows in the low 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: We’re watching rounds of showers and thunderstorms return Monday into the middle of next week as a broader trough settles over eastern Canada and the Northeast. Monday brings partly sunny skies and scattered showers and thunderstorms with highs in the upper 70s to lower 80s. Tuesday looks a bit more active, with numerous showers and thunderstorms possible. Temperatures are again in the upper 70s to lower 80s. Additional scattered showers could linger into Wednesday as highs fall back into the 70s.
